videos.title: 46th Annual Sleeping Giant Loppet
videos.transcript:
Despite a delay of an hour and 15
minutes because of highway closures,
hundreds of skiers hit the trails today
for the 46th annual sleeping giant lopet
for a day of races. Organizers say even
with the mixed weather conditions
lately, the trails were in good
condition for the ski festival. The race
had 665 registered skiers and the
biggest category is the 20 kilometer
skate with about 180 skiers. Other races
included were the 8K mini lapit, 20k
Mary Louise Lake loop, 35k tour, and the
50k classic skate and skilon. The event
coordinator for the sleeping giant lapid
says the event is a main attraction for
skiers all over the region and beyond.
>> It's a participation event. You probably
saw as the the the latter waves were
going through. We've got all ages, kids,
all abilities. Uh there's people that
have been doing the lapet probably for
more than 40 years that that are coming
out and still skiing it. I think it's a
really great community event uh that
just you know everybody who loves cross
country skiing. It's it's one of the
flagship events in the city to uh to
come out and and ski. My take of what
the conditions look like it looks like
it'll be a nice fast race for at least
the first couple of hours. Um the
weather is fantastic for skiing right
around zero.
The 50k skate will results will be
posted on Sunday's sports edition.
